questions.q: What is the phase difference between the voltage and current in a purely resistive AC circuit?
An AC circuit contains a resistance of 20 ohms and an inductance of 50 mH. If the AC source has an angular frequency of 100 rad/s, find the impedance of the circuit.
The current in an AC circuit is given by i = 5 sin (100t) A. Find the rms value of the current.
State the advantage of using AC over DC.
questions.answer: AC can be easily transformed to high or low voltages using a transformer.

questions.q: A 100 μF capacitor is connected to a 220 V, 50 Hz AC source. Find the reactance of the capacitor.
Fill in the blank: The power factor of an AC circuit is given by _______________.
An inductor of inductance 100 mH is connected to a 100 V, 50 Hz AC source. Find the current in the inductor.
State True or False: The impedance of an AC circuit is always greater than or equal to the resistance of the circuit.

A series LCR circuit has a resistance of 10 ohms, an inductance of 10 mH, and a capacitance of 100 μF. If the circuit is connected to a 100 V, 50 Hz AC source, find the resonant frequency of the circuit.
An AC circuit has a resistance of 20 ohms and a reactance of 30 ohms. Find the power factor of the circuit.
